report_name: Google Patches 18 Severe Vulnerabilities, Including Four Critical, in Chrome 149 Security Update

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-13028
vulnerabilities.description: A critical use-after-free vulnerability in the WebGL component of Google Chrome, which could be exploited for remote code execution.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-13032
vulnerabilities.description: A critical use-after-free vulnerability in the WebGL component of Google Chrome, which could be exploited for remote code execution.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-13038
vulnerabilities.description: A critical use-after-free vulnerability in the Autofill component of Google Chrome, which could be exploited for remote code execution.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-13033
vulnerabilities.description: A critical out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the Blink InterestGroups component of Google Chrome, which could be exploited for remote code execution.

indicators.name: Google Chrome Spawning Command Shell
indicators.pattern:
title: Google Chrome Spawning Command Shell
id: 9c5b8f8a-1b3a-4f0e-9a1b-3c8d7e6f5a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects a Google Chrome browser process spawning a command shell (cmd.exe or powershell.exe). This is a strong indicator of post-exploitation behavior following a successful remote code execution exploit.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/chrome-149-update-resolves-18-severe-vulnerabilities/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-25
modified: 2026-06-25
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely, but could be triggered by legitimate developer tools or extensions that launch shell processes.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\chrome.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects when the Google Chrome process spawns a command shell (cmd.exe) or PowerShell, which is a common post-exploitation technique after a successful browser exploit.
